facts.text: Chicago is served by O'Hare International Airport, the world's busiest by operations, and Midway International Airport on the Southwest Side.
The 1893 World's Columbian Exposition drew 27.5 million visitors and is considered the most influential world's fair in history.
When Chicago was founded in 1837, most early buildings clustered around the mouth of the Chicago River, as shown on the original 58‑block map.
The Willis Tower, formerly Sears Tower, remains a popular destination for tourists and a symbol of Chicago's skyline.
Chicago is home to the Chicago Symphony Orchestra and the Lyric Opera of Chicago, while the Art Institute of Chicago houses an influential visual arts museum.
Chicago is the birthplace of house music, industrial music, and a vibrant hip‑hop scene that has shaped global electronic dance culture.
